headline: Could revealing Rishi Sunak’s cowboy boots ruin Brex-Tex relations?
summary: There’s a Sunak in my boots!
body_text:
One of Rishi Sunak’s strangest legacies is as a footwear influencer.

From £95 Palm Angels sliders to £490 suede Prada loafers and £150 Timberlands, to allegedly ruining Adidas Sambas, the former British prime minister turned-Goldman Sachs, Anthropic and Microsoft senior advisor (many shoes + many hats) sure does make for a more interesting calceological study than most politicians.

But one of Sunak’s shoe episodes has somehow escaped close scrutiny: his receipt, last year, of some unspecified “footwear” from Texas governor Greg Abbott during a March visit for Tex-Brex trade talks:

One thing we can immediately infer is that these were not cheap boots. Under anti-graft measures employed by the UK Civil Service, gifts worth over £140 are labelled as “Above Limit”, and held on to by the government (presumably in some kind of top-secret vault filled with wine, chocolate, tiaras, rubies, golf clubs and football shirts).

However, the intended recipient can purchase the gift back from the
